description: Issue #185: Unicode Bidirectional (BiDi) text support for Arabic/Hebrew

userStories.title: Integrate Unicode BiDi algorithm for RTL text detection
userStories.description: Use the unicode-bidi crate to determine per-character text direction for Arabic, Hebrew, and mixed BiDi content. RTL characters should be tagged with direction=Rtl so the word extractor can group them correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: unicode-bidi crate is integrated for BiDi level resolution
Arabic/Hebrew characters are correctly tagged with direction=Rtl
userStories.notes: The unicode-bidi crate implements UAX #9 (Unicode Bidirectional Algorithm). Key steps: 1) After char extraction, run BiDi algorithm on each line's text, 2) Assign per-char direction based on BiDi levels (even=LTR, odd=RTL), 3) Update char.direction field. This feeds into the per-char direction detection from #181.

userStories.title: RTL word grouping and Arabic shaping
userStories.description: Implement RTL word grouping in the word extractor — characters with direction=Rtl should be grouped right-to-left. Handle Arabic contextual forms (initial, medial, final, isolated) and diacritical mark combining.
acceptanceCriteria: RTL characters are grouped into words right-to-left
Arabic diacritics are combined with their base characters
userStories.notes: Arabic shaping: Unicode characters have contextual forms (U+FE80-U+FEFC) that map to base letters. Diacritical marks (U+0610-U+061A, U+064B-U+065F) should be combined with preceding base chars. The word extractor needs to handle RTL runs within should_split_horizontal by checking chars in reverse x-order.
